analyst_notes: Raw delta is 0.0pp but this is misleading. Two assertions are non-discriminating: (1) 'd3js.org' domain check — skill specifies cdn.jsdelivr.net which is equally valid, causing with-skill to fail while without-skill accidentally passes by using d3js.org; (2) 'branch' keyword — both implementations use branching paths but neither uses the literal word 'branch'. The DISCRIMINATING assertions clearly favor with-skill: (a) Green accent #66BB6A — with-skill consistently uses it (3/3), without-skill never does (0/3, using blue/purple instead); (b) Mobile 280px proportions — with-skill uses exact spec (160x280), without-skill uses arbitrary sizes (240x480). When only counting discriminating assertions, with-skill wins +2 assertions per eval on average. Recommend fixing non-discriminating assertions for iteration 2.
